title: -Contract No. 32600081 Amendment: Metro Elevator for Maintenance, Repairs and Emergency Response Services for Citywide Elevators

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32600081 with Metro Elevator to provide maintenance, repairs, and emergency
response services to increase the contract amount by $175,000 for a total not to exceed
$350,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Budgets include funding in FY 2026, distributed across the General Fund (011),
Sanitary Sewer Fund (611), Off-Street Parking Fund (627), Equipment Maintenance
Fund (672), and Building Maintenance Fund (673) to cover the cost of elevator
maintenance, repairs and emergency response services for citywide elevators. Funding
for future years is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed citywide budget for
Fiscal Years 2027-2028.
sections.current_situation:
Elevator maintenance, repairs and emergency response services are critical to
maintaining the safety, reliability, and operational needs of the City’s elevators.
The City issued Request for Proposals (RFP) Specification No. 25-11723-C, with a
submission due date of June 3, 2025. The City received three proposals in response.
The selection committee, composed of subject matter experts from Public Works,
evaluated the proposals.
The evaluation criteria used to rate the proposals were as follows: Project Approach
(30%), Expertise and References (15%), Cost (25%), and Background (30%). Metro
Elevator was awarded a contract as the best and most responsive bidder, which was
executed in December 2025. As a result of the inability to formalize a contract with a
second high scoring service provider from the same RFP process, Dream Ride
Elevator, an amendment is needed at this time to increase the service capacity provided
by Metro Elevator.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Contract Amendment: Metro Elevator for Maintenance, CONSENT CALENDAR
Repairs and Emergency Response Services February 24, 2026
This contract will support the City’s elevators, which are critical to support staff and
community members, and is aligned with the Strategic Plan Priority of advancing our
goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
The City currently utilizes 30 elevators located in 16 facilities. Proper maintenance,
repair and emergency response services for City elevators supports the safety and
accessibility of staff and community members, while also complying with state and
federal safety requirements.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identified environmental sustainability or climate impacts of executing these
contracts.
sections.rationale:
Regular inspection and maintenance of elevators is required to meet safety
regulations. These contracts will ensure compliance with state and local codes and
improve responsiveness to emergency calls.
sections.alternatives:
The City does not currently have staff with the certification and experience needed to
provide elevator maintenance and repair services for the City in-house. As a result,
contracting remains the only viable option.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, (510) 981-6629
Aaron Baker, Facilities Maintenance Superintendent, (510) 981-6452
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32600081 AMENDMENT: METRO ELEVATOR FOR MAINTENANCE,
REPAIRS AND EMERGENCY RESPONSE SERVICES FOR CITYWIDE ELEVATORS
WHEREAS, the City conducted RFP Solicitation No. 25-11723-C with bids due no later
than June 3, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the City received three proposals and a selection committee, composed of
subject matter experts from the Public Works Facilities Division and the Public Works
Operations Division, met to evaluate the bids; and
WHEREAS, Metro Elevator was selected as the most responsive bidder and was
awarded the contract; and
WHEREAS, Metro Elevator and the City executed Contract No. 32600081, which was
approved by Council on July 27, 2025, through Resolution 71,932-N.S. for an amount not
to exceed $175,000; and
WHEREAS, an increased need for additional elevator service has been identified by Staff;
due to an inability to formalize a contract with a secondary service provider; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the Fiscal Year 2026 adopted budget in General Fund
(011), Off-Street Parking Fund (627), Sanitary Sewer Fund (611), Equipment
Maintenance Fund (672), and Building Maintenance Fund (673). Funding for future years
is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed citywide budget for Fiscal Years 2027-
2028.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend Contract No. 32600081 with Metro Elevator for
maintenance, repairs, and emergency response services for citywide elevators for three
years from September 1, 2025, to August 31, 2028, for an amount not to exceed $350,000
with the option to extend for two additional one-year terms. Rates adjusted annually in
accordance with the Consumer Price Index.
